column should mention the version of the product to which the change applies.

I INTRODUCTION  
 
I.1 Product name 
 
Product name Dataset name 
OMI_HEALTH_CHL_MEDSEA_OceanColour_trend omi_health_chl_medsea_oceanclour_trend 
OMI_HEALTH_CHL_MEDSEA_OceanColour_area_averaged_mean omi_health_chl_medsea_oceanclour_area_averaged_mean 
OMI_HEALTH_CHL_MEDSEA_OceanColour_area_averaged_anomaly N/A 
 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
 
The CMEMS Ocean Colour OMIs are derived from the reprocessed (MY) regional gridded  
chlorophyll products generated and distributed by the CMEMS OC -TAC. The input dataset are  
spectral remote sensing reflectances (Rrs) derived from multi-sensor (SeaStar-SeaWiFS, AQUA- 
MODIS, NOAA20 -VIIRS, NPP -VIIRS, Envisat -MERIS and Sentinel3 -OLCI) (at 1 km resolution)  
processor and produced by CNR using an in-house processing chain. To date, data are based on 
R2022.0 processing for NASA/NOAA sensors (SeaStar -SeaWiFS, AQUA-MODIS, NOAA20-VIIRS, 
NPP-VIIRS), 3rd reprocessing for Envisat-MERIS and baseline collection 3.01 for Sentinel3-OLCI. 
Chlorophyll product is obtained by means of the Mediterranean Ocean Colour regional 
algorithms: an updated version of the MedOC4 (Case 1 (off -shore) waters, Volpe et al., 2019,  
with new coefficients) and AD4 (Case 2 (coastal) waters, Bertho n and Zibordi, 2004). The  
processing chain and the techniques used for algorithms merging are detailed in the 
corresponding QUID (Colella et al., 2023). 
The MY regional chlorophyll dataset was used to compute anomaly map, with respect to  
reference climatology (1997-2014), daily and monthly regional mean time series and trend map. 
Trend map was obtained by applying Colella et al. (2016) methodology. Monthly chlorophyll  
maps, derived from MY data, are used to compute the trend for the period 1997 -2023. Trend 
analysis has been performed by using the X -11 seasonal adjustment procedure (Pezzulli et al.  
2005). The X-11 procedure assumes that the CHL time series Yt can be decomposed into its three 
components: the seasonal signal St, the inter-annual component Tt, and an irregular component 
It: 
Yt = Tt + St + It 
The deseasonalized time series is obtained through an iterative process (Colella et al., 2016), by 
using successive filters, fro
